requirements.statement: Providers that do not implement vault-style semantics shall not be required to emulate protected memory in order to satisfy the canonical provider contract.
Protected or exact-preservation memory semantics shall live in governance capability metadata or provider-direct APIs rather than in the required provider core.

The common runtime shall expose governance behavior only when the operation is broadly reusable, leaving more specialized protected-memory controls to provider-direct surfaces until they justify canonical treatment.
The canonical remember/3 path shall remain the shared minimal write operation for structured memory attrs even as richer providers add more advanced ingestion flows.
Batch, multimodal, or routed ingestion flows shall enter through an optional ingestion capability family or provider-direct APIs rather than by changing the required core write contract.
The common memory plugin shall remain core-memory oriented and shall not require every provider to expose advanced ingestion routes through the shared agent-facing plugin surface.

The provider architecture shall classify advanced features into required core, optional capability, or provider-direct lanes so new providers can add behavior without redefining the canonical contract.
The required core lane shall stay limited to cross-provider operations that minimal and advanced providers can all satisfy without emulating specialized subsystems.
Provider-direct APIs shall remain a valid lane for specialized controls that are useful for a specific provider but not yet justified as canonical runtime operations.
The canonical retrieve/3 contract shall continue returning the shared record-oriented result shape even when a provider internally performs active planning or routed retrieval.

Retrieval plans, routing traces, and other provider-native diagnostic details shall be exposed through explainability metadata or provider-direct APIs rather than a new canonical retrieve/3 result format.
Provider-specific retrieval controls shall be carried through optional hints or extensions rather than mandatory additions to the base Jido.Memory.Query contract.
The plugin shall support auto-capture of configured signal patterns into structured memory records.
The default shipped memory path shall be ETS-backed and use ETS as the authoritative store unless the caller supplies another store implementation.
The package shall provide a Jido plugin and explicit actions for remember, recall, and forget flows.
The package shall provide structured record and query contracts plus a runtime facade and store behavior for writing, retrieving, and deleting memory records.

The proposal shall make jido_memory the package that ships the standard provider choices needed for unified memory context management in Jido, instead of requiring a second library for the common advanced path.
The canonical provider architecture proposal shall place the shared provider contract in jido_memory rather than in an implementation-specific advanced library.
The proposal shall separate required core provider operations from optional capability behaviors so minimal and advanced providers can coexist without a single oversized interface.

The proposal shall treat the current runtime and store stack as the Basic provider path, position a native Tiered provider in jido_memory as the standard built-in advanced path, and leave jido_memory_os as a standalone advanced library with optional future interop rather than a required core dependency.

New advanced provider features shall enter the canonical layer additively through optional capability families or provider-native extensions rather than by enlarging the required provider core with specialized concerns.
Providers may expose retrieval plans, routing traces, and other provider-native explanation details through explainability metadata without changing the canonical retrieve/3 result shape.

Protected or exact-preservation memory semantics, such as vault-style storage, shall be modeled through governance capabilities or provider-direct APIs rather than required behavior for all providers.
The capability model shall support a distinct ingestion-oriented capability family so batch, multimodal, and routed write flows can be negotiated separately from the core remember/3 contract.
Advanced memory features shall be expressed as optional capability behaviours separate from the required provider core contract.
Providers shall expose capabilities as structured metadata rather than an untyped flat list so tooling and facades can reason about supported features.
The canonical layer shall return a typed unsupported-capability error when a caller requests a capability the configured provider does not implement.

Providers shall own their own bootstrap boundary through core callbacks so advanced implementations can attach supervision and provider metadata without leaking implementation-specific startup details into the facade.

jido_memory shall expose a stable built-in provider catalog for the common memory strategies it owns, so users can select those providers without importing additional libraries for the standard Jido memory path.

Applications shall select memory implementations as provider bundles exposed through jido_memory, whether those bundles point at built-in providers or explicit external modules, rather than wiring low-level implementation details directly into agent code.

The canonical provider layer shall define a required core behaviour that covers configuration validation, child spec declaration, initialization, capability reporting, and the base remember, get, retrieve, forget, prune, and info operations.

The required core provider behaviour shall remain small and cross-provider so specialized concerns such as multimodal ingestion, memory-type routing, retrieval planning, and protected vault semantics do not become mandatory callbacks for all providers.

The common plugin and runtime configuration shall allow library users to select built-in provider choices shipped by jido_memory without changing agent memory calls or depending on a second library for the standard advanced path.
Actions and runtime-facing helpers shall dispatch through a canonical memory facade rather than calling provider-specific modules directly.

The common runtime facade shall stay selective, standardizing only broadly reusable operations while leaving provider-specific manager controls, retrieval plans, and specialized workflows to capability-specific helpers, info payloads, or provider-direct APIs.

Jido.Memory.Plugin shall be the unified memory-context entrypoint for Jido agents and shall be provider-configurable so the same agent-facing plugin and action API can target different memory implementations.

Provider-specific retrieval or ingestion features shall travel through optional hints, explainability payloads, or provider-direct APIs so the shared plugin and runtime surface stays stable across basic, tiered, and more specialized providers.

Jido.Memory.Record and the base Jido.Memory.Query contract shall remain canonical, while provider-specific retrieval features stay in backward-compatible options or hints rather than mandatory core fields.
The first provider implementation shall wrap the existing Jido.Memory.Runtime and Jido.Memory.Store path so current ETS-backed behavior remains the default.
The provider architecture shall be introduced additively so existing runtime flows, record and store contracts, and default plugin usage continue to work while applications migrate incrementally.
jido_memory_os shall remain a standalone advanced library with its native facade and plugin rather than becoming a required dependency of jido_memory for standard memory context management.
The common advanced memory path for Jido shall be implemented as a built-in Tiered provider inside jido_memory rather than requiring jido_memory_os as the standard provider choice.
The repository shall include a .spec/AGENTS.md that gives local operating guidance for agents editing current-truth subjects.
Current-truth subject specs shall live in .spec/specs/, while supporting RFCs and design notes shall stay outside that directory.
The repository shall include a .spec/decisions/README.md that explains when durable ADRs belong in the workspace.
The repository shall include a .spec/README.md that explains the purpose, layout, and workflow for the local Spec Led workspace.

given: a provider that does not support protected memory semantics
then: it remains valid without implementing vault-style behavior
when: the provider is evaluated against the canonical contract

given: a provider that stores exact-preservation facts separately from general memory
then: the protected-memory semantics are expressed through governance metadata or provider-direct APIs without enlarging the required provider core
when: the provider is integrated through the canonical layer

given: a provider that supports only the canonical memory write path
then: the provider succeeds through remember/3 without needing any ingestion-specific API surface
when: an application writes structured memory attrs

given: a provider that accepts batches of multimodal input and routes them internally across memory subsystems
then: the canonical write path remains available while the richer ingestion path is advertised and invoked through an optional ingestion lane
when: the provider is integrated through the canonical layer

given: a minimal provider that only supports the canonical memory operations
then: it satisfies the required core lane without needing optional capability or provider-direct behavior
when: the provider is classified against the matrix

given: a specialized provider with active retrieval planning, multimodal ingestion, and protected memory
then: its common operations remain in the required core lane while its specialized controls enter through optional capabilities or provider-direct APIs
when: the provider is classified against the matrix

given: a provider that generates an internal retrieval plan before selecting memory sources
then: any plan-specific controls remain optional hints or provider-direct features rather than becoming mandatory base query fields
when: the provider is used through the canonical runtime surface

given: a provider that adds tier-aware explanation details to canonical retrieval
then: the explanation contains provider-native context while retrieve/3 continues returning the shared record-oriented result shape
when: a caller asks for retrieval explanation

given: two agents write memory without overriding namespace
then: each agent sees only its own namespace-scoped records
when: each agent recalls its own memories

given: a mounted plugin with matching capture patterns
then: the signal is persisted as a structured memory record
when: the agent receives a matching signal

given: a configured provider that supports core retrieval but not explainable retrieval
then: the request fails with a typed unsupported-capability error rather than a silent no-op
when: a caller asks the canonical layer for retrieval explanation

given: a provider that supports batch multimodal ingestion in addition to the canonical core write path
then: ingestion support is discoverable through a dedicated optional capability family instead of changing the core remember/3 contract
when: tooling inspects the provider capability map

given: a provider that includes protected exact-preservation memory
then: the protected memory semantics are represented through governance capability metadata or provider-direct APIs instead of becoming a mandatory part of the shared core provider contract
when: the provider advertises its advanced feature set

given: a provider that performs active retrieval planning and memory-type routing
then: the provider can return routing traces and planning metadata as explainability output without changing the canonical retrieve/3 return shape
when: the caller asks for retrieval explanation through the canonical layer

given: a built-in Tiered provider that supports lifecycle and tier-aware retrieval features
then: the supported lifecycle and retrieval features are discoverable as structured metadata without changing the core provider contract
when: tooling inspects the provider capability map

given: a default Basic provider configuration backed by the current runtime and store path
then: the provider returns core capability metadata and any startup requirements through the required callbacks
when: the provider is initialized through the canonical contract

given: an optional external provider that needs its own supervised components
then: provider metadata and child specs enter through provider callbacks rather than plugin-specific special cases
when: the canonical facade resolves and initializes that provider

given: a specialized provider with multimodal ingestion, routed retrieval, and protected memory concepts
then: it satisfies the shared core callbacks while exposing its specialized behavior through optional capabilities or provider-direct APIs rather than expanding the required provider contract
when: the provider is integrated through the canonical layer

given: a built-in Tiered provider configuration with short, mid, and long memory concerns
then: the provider reports its tiered capability metadata and any startup requirements through the same core callback surface
when: the provider is initialized through the canonical contract

given: a plugin configuration that selects the built-in Tiered provider
then: the agent-facing API remains stable while tiered behavior stays behind the canonical facade and shared record and query contracts
when: agent memory actions are executed

given: a plugin configuration that does not explicitly select a provider
then: the default Basic provider path preserves the current ETS-backed experience behind the canonical facade
when: the plugin is mounted for an agent

given: a plugin configuration that selects an explicit external provider bundle
then: the same plugin and runtime surface dispatch through the canonical facade without introducing provider-specific call sites into agent code
when: agent memory actions are executed

given: a specialized provider that offers multimodal ingestion, active retrieval planning, or protected memory
then: shared calls remain stable while specialized behavior is exposed through capability-specific helpers, explainability output, info payloads, or provider-direct APIs
when: the provider is used through the common plugin and runtime surface for shared memory operations

given: an application that later adopts the built-in Tiered provider
then: the application gains tiered behavior without changing its agent-facing memory API or taking on a second library as the standard advanced dependency
when: the application enables tiered memory through the shared plugin and runtime surface

given: an application that uses the current default ETS-backed memory flow
then: the application continues to work through the Basic provider without requiring provider-specific migration
when: the provider architecture lands

given: an application that wants the standalone MemoryOS control plane and native workflows
then: it continues to use MemoryOS through its native facade and plugin without forcing jido_memory to depend on that library for the common provider story
when: the application adopts jido_memory_os directly

meta.summary: Draft matrix for governance and protected-memory features in pluggable memory providers.
Draft matrix for core writes versus optional ingestion capabilities in pluggable memory providers.
Draft matrix for classifying memory provider features into required core, optional capability, and provider-direct lanes.
Draft matrix for canonical retrieval, explainability traces, and provider-native query extensions.
jido_memory provides a structured record model, query model, store abstraction, ETS-backed runtime, and plugin/actions integration for Jido agents.

Draft provider architecture for making jido_memory the unified Jido memory package with built-in provider choices such as Basic and Tiered, while leaving jido_memory_os as a standalone advanced library.
Draft capability model for optional memory provider behaviors, discovery, and unsupported-capability handling.
Draft core provider contract for configuration-driven pluggable memory providers in jido_memory, including built-in provider choices.
Draft facade contract for provider-configurable plugins, canonical dispatch, and shared record and query types.
Draft migration path for introducing the provider model, preserving current jido_memory behavior, and adding a built-in Tiered provider while leaving jido_memory_os standalone.
Local workspace contract for authored specs and supporting design boundaries in jido_memory.
